description:
In `packages/core/src/handlers/tokens.ts`, `parseTokens` does not validate the structure of the token file. Malformed token files (wrong shape, missing required fields) cause silent data loss or confusing downstream errors.

**Fix:** Define a Zod schema for the W3C DTCG token format and run `TokenSchema.parse(...)` on the loaded JSON before returning. Throw a structured `MCPError(INVALID_INPUT, ...)` if validation fails.

**Acceptance criteria:**
- A Zod schema validates the token file structure
- Malformed token file produces a clear `[INVALID_INPUT]` error with a helpful message
- Valid token files continue to parse correctly
- Tests cover the malformed-file error path
title: fix: validate design token files against a Zod schema

coderabbitai: **Actionable comments posted: 2** > [!CAUTION] > Some comments are outside the diff and can’t be posted inline due to platform limitations. > > > >
> ⚠️ Outside diff range comments (1)
> >
> packages/core/src/handlers/tokens.ts (1)
> > `78-85`: _🧹 Nitpick_ | _🔵 Trivial_ > > **Consider removing the `[INVALID_INPUT]` prefix from the error message.** > > The category is already conveyed via `ErrorCategory.INVALID_INPUT`. Including it in the message creates redundancy and couples the message format to the category enum. Error handlers that need the category can access `mcpError.category` directly. > >
> ♻️ Suggested simplification > > ```diff > throw new MCPError( > - `[INVALID_INPUT] Tokens file has invalid structure: ${filePath} — ${issues}`, > + `Tokens file has invalid structure: ${filePath} — ${issues}`, > ErrorCategory.INVALID_INPUT, > ); > ``` >
> >
> 🤖 Prompt for AI Agents > > ``` > Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed. > > In `@packages/core/src/handlers/tokens.ts` around lines 78 - 85, Remove the > redundant "[INVALID_INPUT]" prefix from the thrown MCPError message in the > TokenFileSchema validation block: after TokenFileSchema.safeParse(parsed) when > !validated.success, build the error message using only the filePath and the > issues string (the variable issues derived from validated.error.issues) and > throw new MCPError with that message and ErrorCategory.INVALID_INPUT as before; > keep using MCPError and ErrorCategory.INVALID_INPUT but avoid coupling the > textual message to the enum value. > ``` > >
